text:
in this video we'll be covering 
a unique complete strategy that

most Traders aren't aware of this is a break 
and retest setup we'll go over the strategy

criteria and give you various examples so 
you can truly understand how and why this

works let's start off with a quick summary we'll 
cover the basics of the break and retest strategy

including what it is and how it works next we'll 
go over identifying key levels which includes

areas of Confluence like support resistance 
Fibonacci and fair value gaps then we'll dive

into the break and retest criteria which includes 
Trend following momentum and Confluence lastly

we'll go over various examples of the break and 
retest setups so you can recognize this pattern on

your own to make better trades the first thing you 
should know is what exactly is a break and retest

and what does it look like if you couldn't already 
tell by the name of this setup it starts with a

breakout at a certain level then following that 
breakout it retests that previous level this is

where the trade opportunities present themselves 
the main idea of this setup is basically to enter

on an old resistance once the market has flipped 
it to support now it's very important that once

the market starts coming back for a retest you 
want to enter only if it gives a good reaction

this can be a certain Candlestick pattern or 
a series of Candlestick patterns indicating

that price action is aligning with the breakout 
Direction this pattern occurs frequently in the

markets and can be found almost anywhere across 
all time frames and assets but most importantly

it's one of the simplest trade setups in 
existence and one of the most successful

and that's why so many new Traders love using it 
and I think you will too moving on we'll show you

how to identify these key levels where breaks 
and retest opportunities arise so on this chart

we can see an uptrending Market as it's making 
consecutive higher highs and higher lows but if

you look closely you can notice that the market 
has previously retested these highs right here it

respected the level and moved up higher now since 
it happened once we can expect more opportunities

like this in the near future so assuming that the 
market is still bullish you want to Mark out this

old High that has now potentially shifted to 
our new support level in addition you should

also look for other signs of support pull out 
your FIB retracement tool and select the swing

low that broke this High then proceed to select 
the highest high as your second Pivot Point what

you'll see here is that the old highs tend to line 
up together within the golden Fibonacci pocket and

we can also include this fair value Gap as a form 
of support this is exactly what Confluence is and

you should always be looking for it in trading 
basically various factors that support the trading

idea you're proposing once you've identified your 
key level you want to wait for price to return to

this area and enter once it shows bullish price 
action so as the market reaches to this area we

can see that it reacts from it and moves up higher 
but we still want it to create a series of higher

highs and higher lows before we enter the position 
this all comes down to patience a Trader's best

friend now that the market has finally touched 
our key level for the third time and moved up

past this swing High it has shifted from barish 
back to bullish another indication of bullish

momentum is this fair value Gap right here this 
candle is way larger than the ones before it so

with the bullish momentum back in Action enter on 
the retest of the local swing High set your stop

at the low and Target your levels higher now that 
you understand the idea of a break and retest you

need to know the criteria and that starts with 
defining what a breakout actually is a breakout

is when price moves above a resistance level or 
even below a support level with conviction indic

ating a new potential Trend now there may 
be times when you have this exact scenario

of price breaking out but then nothing actually 
happens it doesn't continue in that direction in

that scenario Traders are trapped in underwater 
positions but why does this happen it could be

that it's just a liquidity grab as fuel to move 
back in the original Direction that's why we need

to have our criteria met for the breakouts to be 
valid giving us a higher probability of making

a successful trade our first criteria is to only 
take trades in the direction of the current Trend

if it's bearish that means we're waiting for 
the market to break past a swing low this is

potentially turned into a new resistance level and 
it's an opportunity to look for a short position

the second criteria is that when the move breaks 
past the level it should be with a lot of energy

meaning it has to be a candle or a series of 
candles that's larger than the ones before it

this shows you that momentum of the current trend 
is there and you can be confident in your trade

playing out now the final and most important part 
is to find Confluence so we need other forms or

other signals of resistance besides just another 
swing low for example this could be a Fibonacci

level a point of control a moving average really 
anything and when all of this criteria has met

you're ready to take a trade and watch it play out 
let's put this all together and we're going to go

over some real examples for you here we have the 
gold chart you'll notice that the market has been

ranging for quite a while we also have equal 
highs right here which is where liquidity is

likely resting so price does have a reason to go 
up higher and that reason is to take out the highs

after a while it started moving up with a series 
of bullish candlesticks creating higher highs as

price retraced back down it finds support at this 
old swing High which previously was a resistance

level so knowing that the market is bullish again 
we want to look for long opportunities here we can

see the market breaking above a swing high with a 
large energetic bullish Candlestick your favorite

it then engulfed the bearish candle next to it and 
made a small bullish fair value Gap so now we want

to select our FIB retracement tool and try to find 
some kind of Confluence select the swing low that

broke the high and the most relevant highest high 
here you'll see that the 0.5 level aligns with our

support when the market retraces back we'll look 
to enter a long position with this Confluence all

we have to do is set our stop below the low and 
Target our levels higher now let me give you an

example in a bearish market we can see that here 
as price is making lower highs and lower lows

consecutively and here it has created a large 
Fair value Gap leaving behind an inefficiency

so price we know will likely come back to fill 
it again take out your FIB tool and place it on

the swing high and low to find resistance the 
618 lines up with our resistance level and our

fair value Gap so this is our key level and when 
price returns to it wait for bearish Price action

like an engulfing Candlestick and then look to 
enter set your stop above the highs and Target

those sweet G gains lower the Breakin retest 
strategy offers a structured approach to trading

breakouts giving you a higher probability setup 
by focusing on key levels waiting for confirmed

breakouts and managing your trades carefully you 
can increase your chances of successful trading

that's the whole video guys I hope you enjoyed 
it if you know a Trader that might benefit from

this knowledge please share it with them check out 
the rest of our free educational trading playlist

on the channel and make sure to smash 
the likes and subscribes on your way out
